description: Live external-tool sync snapshot for the FitMe PM framework. Captures the current state of GitHub, Linear, Notion, Vercel, and analytics/observability so the dashboard can compare repo truth against workspace truth without relying on narrative docs alone.

findings: Repo is on main, aligned with origin/main, working tree clean (all prior session work committed 2026-04-13).
3 commits landed: auth hardening (ece89bd), dashboard control room (c2c38fa), framework v4.3 + docs (2793b01).
Dashboard GitHub issue hydration still depends on GITHUB_TOKEN at build time — repo-local truth only.
iOS simulator build succeeded (iPhone 17 Pro, iOS 26.4) — auth wiring compile-verified.
The roadmap project itself is still marked Backlog even though active maintenance and control-room work is underway.
The active maintenance stack now exists explicitly in Linear: FIT-6, FIT-17, FIT-21, FIT-22, FIT-23, FIT-24, and FIT-25 are all in progress.
Most legacy roadmap issues still remain Todo despite materially more shipped progress in the repo.
Linear setup/tutorial issues FIT-1 through FIT-4 are still open and add noise to planning views.

tracked_issues.title: Run maintenance sync: source-of-truth, design/UX, marketing, and Vercel cleanup
Build operations control room dashboard for product, UX, design, analytics, and PM
Replace mock-backed auth with production Supabase auth
Create production-quality app icon and App Store asset system
Define push notifications strategy and first implementation scope
Research import training plan feature for external sources and AI-generated plans
Research AI engine architecture adaptation using PM-flow patterns

tracked_pages.title: Case Study — Maintenance Cleanup + Operations Control Room

findings: The Maintenance Audit, control-room spec, auth-hardening chapter, and auth runtime verification playbook are aligned with repo truth and current cleanup work.

Project Context & Status now has a v4.3 truth anchor and updated next-step priorities, but it still retains legacy historical sections that should be reconciled over time rather than treated as current truth.
The canonical fit-tracker2 Vercel project now serves the current operations control room, including the new knowledge hub, at fit-tracker2.vercel.app.
The canonical fit-tracker2 Vercel project is still configured with `rootDirectory: dashboard`, so the repo's marketing website is not the truthfully launched public surface yet.
Web Analytics and Speed Insights are now deployed on the canonical fit-tracker2 project.
The marketing website code still has launch blockers, including placeholder GA4 IDs, placeholder App Store links, and unverified aggregate-rating metadata.
A separate dashboard Vercel project still exists from the earlier accidental deploy and should be archived or removed once we decide cleanup policy.
Runtime logs remain empty for both projects, so deeper production observability is still limited.
The analytics framework is strong on paper: 40 metrics are defined and 14 are available immediately from current instrumentation.
Instrumentation coverage is still only 35%, and retention, CX, and revenue-facing metrics remain blocked by Firebase setup, Task 14, Task 15, and external services.

Web Analytics and Speed Insights are now deployed on the canonical dashboard surface, but runtime analytics and crash observability are still not wired strongly enough to treat health-status telemetry as production-grade.
